title: A multi-omics approach unravels metagenomic and metabolic alterations of a probiotic and synbiotic additive in rainbow trout (Oncorhynchus mykiss)

paper_analysis.summary: This paper studies how probiotic and synbiotic feed additives alter the gut microbiome and metabolome of rainbow trout using a multi-omics workflow combining 16S rRNA profiling, deep shotgun metagenomics, and untargeted UHPLC-MS/MS plus IC HR-MS/MS metabolomics. The main findings are that probiotic/synbiotic feeds reduce the dominance of Candidatus Mycoplasma salmoninae, shift microbial functional pathways (notably arginine biosynthesis and terpenoid backbone synthesis), and are associated with distinct changes in intestinal metabolites including lipids, amino acids, bile acids, and steroid-related compounds.
measurement_types: 16S rRNA gene amplicon sequencing (V3–V4 gut microbiome profiling)
feed composition analysis by near-infrared spectroscopy
growth/performance measurements (weight gain, feed conversion ratio, feed efficiency ratio, lipid efficiency ratio, protein efficiency ratio)
qPCR quantification of 16S rRNA gene regions

figures.description: Growth and feed-performance metrics across the three diets: weight gain, feed efficiency ratio, lipid efficiency ratio, and protein efficiency ratio, with pairwise statistical comparisons.
Feed formulation comparison showing core composition and additives for CTRL, PRO, and SYN diets, including fat, protein, calories, Pediococcus acidilactici MA18/5M, and GOS.
Relative abundance barplots of the most abundant ASVs in mid and distal gut sections across feeding groups.
PCoA of 16S-based microbial composition showing clustering by feed type and gut section.
Hill-number diversity metrics for ASV richness, Shannon diversity, and Simpson diversity across feed types.
Heat tree of differential abundance among the 50 most abundant ASVs for pairwise feed comparisons.
figures.data_type: taxonomic heat tree / differential abundance visualization

figures.description: Rarefaction curves of ORF discovery versus sequencing depth for metagenomic samples.
Tukey HSD coefficient plot comparing COG coverage between feeding groups.
Network of 17,354 COGs and samples showing similarity structure among metagenomes.
Density plot and bar plot of bacterial single-copy genes used to estimate the number of genomes in the metagenome.
MAG abundance across samples plus heatmap of selected genes related to isoprenoid biosynthesis, arginine biosynthesis, and polymer utilization.
Volcano plot of differentially abundant metagenomic genes between CTRL and additive diets.
PCoA of metabolite profiles across diets for all metabolites and selected chemical classes (amino acids/peptides, terpenoids, bile acids/alcohols), with point opacity linked to Mycoplasma abundance.
Volcano plot of differential intensity for 240 known metabolites with spectral matches, comparing CTRL against PRO and SYN.
Molecular network of bacterial-associated metabolites (BAMs) and related molecular families, annotated with MolNetEnhancer, SIRIUS+CSI:FingerID, MS2LDA motifs, and hypothetical structures.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: Yes. The paper reports raw experimental measurements from fish growth trials, 16S amplicon sequencing reads from 240 gut-content samples, deep shotgun metagenomic sequencing from 12 gut-content samples (six individuals, mid and distal gut; >1.5 Tb raw sequence data), untargeted metabolomics MS/MS data from gut-content samples (22,222 mass spectral features with tandem MS data), qPCR quantification of 16S regions, and feed composition measurements by near-infrared spectroscopy. The text does not specify file formats for deposited raw files, but it clearly describes raw sequencing and mass-spectrometry acquisition.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The provided text includes a Creative Commons license statement and says the data are made available in the article unless otherwise stated, but it does not include a dedicated data availability section or repository accession numbers in the excerpt. No explicit download link, accession, or repository is visible in the supplied text.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data described in the paper include sequencing reads for 16S rRNA amplicons and shotgun metagenomics, raw MS/MS spectra/features from UHPLC-MS/MS and IC HR-MS/MS, qPCR measurements, and feed composition measurements. The excerpt does not specify exact file formats, repository names, or accession IDs.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include ASVs after filtering low-abundance and rare taxa, rarefaction curves, Hill-number diversity metrics, differential abundance results, co-assembled contigs, ORFs, COG annotations, SCGs, MAGs, gene coverage tables, metabolite feature tables, filtered metabolite sets, spectral-library matches, and molecular-network annotations. Tables S2/S3 and figures summarize these processed outputs.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-ready data are clearly implied for all main figures: abundance tables, ordination coordinates, diversity metrics, heat-tree inputs, rarefaction curves, metagenomic coverage matrices, volcano-plot statistics, metabolomic feature matrices, and molecular-network node/edge annotations. The excerpt does not explicitly state that source data files are deposited separately, but the figures are based on processed numerical datasets.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: The excerpt mentions analysis workflows and software methods such as MolNetEnhancer, SIRIUS+CSI:FingerID, MS2LDA, GNPS, HMM-based SCG analysis, and statistical tests (Wilcoxon, Benjamini-Hochberg, Tukey HSD), but it does not mention custom scripts, notebooks, or code repositories.
dataset_characterization.notes: The study is multi-modal and strongly processed: raw sequencing and MS data are transformed into ASVs, contigs/ORFs/COGs/MAGs, and metabolite feature tables before downstream statistics and network annotation. The metagenome is low biomass and host-DNA rich, so deep sequencing was used; the metabolomics dataset is feature-based rather than fully identified compounds, with many unknowns annotated through molecular networking. The paper repeatedly distinguishes between known compounds with spectral matches and unknown metabolites inferred by network-based annotation.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The methods explicitly describe collection of raw 16S amplicon sequencing, deep metagenomic sequencing (>1.5 Tb raw sequence data), untargeted UHPLC-MS/MS and IC HR-MS/MS data, qPCR, and feed composition measurements.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The results and figure captions rely on processed outputs such as ASVs, rarefaction curves, COGs, MAGs, differential abundance tables, metabolite feature tables, and molecular networks, which are figure-ready source data.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: Key phrases in the excerpt: 'data made available in this article', '16S rRNA gene profiling', 'whole-genome metagenomics sequencing', 'untargeted metabolomics', 'more than 1.5 Tb of raw sequence data', '22,222 mass spectral features', 'MolNetEnhancer', 'SIRIUS+CSI:FingerID', 'GNPS', 'MS2LDA'.
